expectations.text: Flags listRunsWithFindings' per-run loop as an N+1 query pattern
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials independently identified the loop as a textbook N+1 pattern with the same 1+N round-trip framing.

expectations.text: Recommends replacing the loop with a relational `with` query (and notes relations() must be defined)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials proposed db.query.digestRuns.findMany({ with: { findings: true } }) and explicitly noted relations() must first be defined between digestRuns and digestFindings, tying the fix to the missing relations() in schema.ts.

expectations.text: Flags completeRun's update-then-insert as two dependent writes not wrapped in a transaction
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials called out the exact bug, several explicitly noting the code's own comment already documents it, and gave the correct db.transaction(async (tx) => ...) fix.
All 5 trials cited the 'chunk large batch inserts' guidance and recommended batching.

expectations.text: Flags .returning() on a MySQL insert as unsupported
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged this as a hard/critical bug, several quoting the skill's own dialect-compatibility warning verbatim.
All 5 trials caught that a function named 'active members' leaks soft-deleted rows.

expectations.text: Flags invites.invitedBy's references(users.id) instead of () => users.id
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the missing arrow-function form, citing the skill's own FK constraint warning; several contrasted it with inviteAuditLog.inviteId's correct usage two lines below.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not raise a correctness complaint about getInvitesPage's limit/offset pagination
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials treated the limit/offset mechanism itself as correct; at most they suggested cursor pagination as an optional enhancement per the skill's own preference, never as a bug.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not raise a correctness complaint about revokeInvite's db.transaction usage
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly called out revokeInvite's transaction as correct/exemplary and said 'no changes needed'.

expectations.text: Flags comments.parentId's self-reference for missing the (): AnyPgColumn => return type
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials cited the skill's own self-referential relation example in references/relations.md and flagged the missing annotation.

expectations.text: Flags migration 0007's NOT NULL column with no DEFAULT as unsafe on a ~42M-row table
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged this as a blocking issue, most noting it will fail outright against existing rows and describing the safe expand/backfill/validate sequence.

expectations.text: Flags users_email_idx (unique index on email) for not excluding soft-deleted rows, so a new signup reusing a soft-deleted email would violate the unique constraint
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials found the adjacent race condition in reactivateOrCreateUser (select-then-insert TOCTOU) and correctly suggested onConflictDoUpdate on email, but none identified the underlying schema-level design flaw: a non-partial unique index on email means a genuinely NEW/different person can never register with an email a departed user still soft-holds. The skill's references/common-patterns.md documents soft-delete queries and references/schema-definition.md documents unique indexes, but neither file - nor SKILL.md - ever discusses combining the two (e.g. a partial/conditional unique index scoped to WHERE deleted_at IS NULL). Without that pattern in the skill, reviewers have no hook to reach for it.
expectations.failure_example: Trial adb04a633638a4cf2's review recommends onConflictDoUpdate({ target: t.users.email, set: { deletedAt: null } }) for reactivateOrCreateUser but never questions whether the unique index itself should be partial/conditional to allow re-registration by an unrelated new user.
expectations.fix_note: Added a 'Soft Delete + Unique Constraints' subsection to references/common-patterns.md (partial/conditional uniqueIndex().on(...).where(sql`deleted_at IS NULL`) pattern, with rationale) plus a cross-reference callout in references/schema-definition.md's Indexes section. Spot-check: 2/2 independent trials re-run against the updated skill now explicitly flag the users.email partial-index gap, citing the new guidance.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not raise a complaint about listActiveUsers' use of isNull(deletedAt)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: No trial criticized the isNull(deletedAt) filter itself; any comments about listActiveUsers were about unrelated concerns (pagination, future moderation_status filtering).

skill_gaps: SKILL_GAP (eval 2, F3) - FIXED: SKILL.md / references/common-patterns.md and references/schema-definition.md never cover combining soft delete with a unique constraint (partial/conditional unique index scoped to non-deleted rows). 0/5 original trials caught the users.email unique-index-vs-soft-delete interaction; all 5 instead fixed the adjacent (also real) race condition without questioning the index design. Fix: added 'Soft Delete + Unique Constraints' guidance to references/common-patterns.md plus a cross-reference in references/schema-definition.md. Spot-check (2 fresh trials, eval 2 prompt, updated skill): 2/2 now flag the partial-index gap explicitly.
fixture_issues: Eval 1 fixture defect (not scored, but noted) - FIXED: schema.ts's emailIdx: { columns: [table.email], unique: true } was invalid Drizzle syntax (should be a uniqueIndex()/index() builder). This was unintentional - all 5 original trials correctly caught it as a 'CRITICAL' bug, which is good signal on the skill's part, but it wasn't one of the seeded violations and somewhat crowded out attention from the intended precision-trap items. Fixed to `uniqueIndex('users_email_idx').on(table.email)`. Spot-check (2 fresh trials, eval 1 prompt, corrected fixture): 2/2 raised no complaint about index syntax, and both explicitly called it 'correct, no change needed'.

Eval 1 fixture defect (not scored) - FIXED: getInvitesPage's docstring claimed 'non-expired, non-revoked' invites but the query only filters revokedAt, not expiresAt - 3/5 original trials caught this docstring/behavior mismatch as an extra finding. Fixed by correcting the docstring to 'outstanding (non-revoked)' to match actual behavior (preserves eval 1's original intent per evals.json, which only grades the pagination mechanism itself as F5, not expiry filtering). Spot-check (2 fresh trials): 2/2 found no docstring/behavior mismatch, and all 4 intended violations (F1-F4) plus both precision checks (F5-F6) still passed.
